title: FilterHtml behaviour in 4.17.0 causes emails containing self closing link tag to be truncated
author: ondravondra
body:
MimeKit 4.17.0

```csharp
const string body =
                """
                <!DOCTYPE html PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01 Transitional//EN" "http://www.w3.org/TR/html4/loose.dtd"><html><head>
                <meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8"><title></title>

                    <meta http-equiv="content-language" content="cs">
                    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial scale=1.0">

                    <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Open+Sans&subset=latin,latin-ext" rel="stylesheet">
                </head>
                <body>SAMPLE TEXT</body></html>
                """;
var htmlToHtml = new HtmlToHtml
{
    FilterHtml = true,
    FilterComments = true
};
htmlToHtml.Convert(body);
```

Everything after the link tag and the link tag is removed.

In HtmlToHtml.cs the link tag triggers:
```
if (FilterHtml && IsUnsafe (ctx.TagId)) {
    ctx.SuppressInnerContent = true;
    ...
```

and because the link tag is self closing the SuppressInnerContent flag stays in the stack each remaining tag is removed.

title: Hardware Acceleration Support
author: tinohager
body:
As far as I can see, the new hardware acceleration support is not yet supported in the test routine. Fortunately, it is currently only used in `Base64Decoder.cs` and `Base64Encoder.cs`.

At present, there is no way to intervene in this behavior because it is hard-coded. I would like to be able to decide for myself whether I want to enable it.

- https://github.com/jstedfast/MimeKit/pull/1214

title: MimeParser Quits Enumerating Messages in Mbox Prematurely
author: gungora
body:
**Describe the bug**
When one of the MIME messages in an Mbox file has a structural error, MimeParser quits at that message without throwing an exception. This gives the impression that the Mbox file was enumerated successfully and completely when there were items beyond the problematic message that were skipped.

The specific structural error where I observed this problem is when an attachment is Base64 encoded on a single line past 5k bytes, and there is no blank line between the headers of that MIME entity and the attachment contents (i.e., two issues).

**Platform:**
 - OS: Windows
 - .NET Runtime: .Net 8
 - MimeKit Version: 4.13.0

**To Reproduce**

1. Enumerate the supplied Mbox file that contains 4 sample messages.
2. The second message has a structural error.
3. MimeParser only parses two messages without an exception and ignores messages 3 and 4.
4. Parsed message 2 is incomplete and does not contain the ending MIME boundary delimiter. This is not unexpected since the message had a structural issue.

**Expected behavior**
MimeParser should throw a format exception at message 2 and proceed with parsing messages 3 and 4.

If it is possible to work around the error in message 2 and parse it completely despite the structural issue, that would be fantastic but I understand if that is not desirable from a performance standpoint.

**Additional context**
I have included a small sample Mbox file to illustrate the issue. Message 2 of 4 has the structural issue I described.

[Test.mbox.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22566269/Test.mbox.txt)

title: MailAddress Validation Issue With Leading Whitespace
author: sbwalker

title: Preserve AS2 content line endings
author: m0a0k0s
body:
Hello. I'm migrating legacy code that handles AS2 messages to MimeKit.
Old version respects the original line ending for content that passes as binary data.
In MimeKit, there's two places: encrypting ApplicationPkcs7Mime.EncryptAsync and signing MultipartSigned.CreateAsync that forces line ending transformation to DOS.
It references rfc2015 or rfc3156, section 5.1 here, but it's for plain text
https://github.com/jstedfast/MimeKit/blob/7042714e0df9091aa7c73547f1c56da4ee9fd94b/MimeKit/Cryptography/MultipartSigned.cs#L146
When creating the message as is, the mdn calculated on original content outside MimeKit does not match with the one calculated with the result message.
I bypass it now by calling the underlying code directly without conversion part. 
This is the same as https://github.com/jstedfast/MimeKit/issues/569, but another direction - creating a message.

title: ArgumentException: 'non-empty set required' when using SignAndEncrypt with TemporarySecureMimeContext
author: Xgleich8
body:
MimeKit Version: 4.13.0
.NET Version: .Net Framework 4.8.1
Platform: Windows 11 x64

Calling MimeMessage.SignAndEncrypt(...) with a properly initialized TemporarySecureMimeContext results in an ArgumentException caused by an empty trust anchor set (tas), despite importing a trusted root certificate.

``` Text
System.ArgumentException: non-empty set required
Parameter name: tas
   at Org.BouncyCastle.Pkix.PkixParameters.SetTrustAnchors(ISet`1 tas)
   at Org.BouncyCastle.Pkix.PkixParameters..ctor(ISet`1 trustAnchors)
   at Org.BouncyCastle.Pkix.PkixBuilderParameters..ctor(ISet`1 trustAnchors, ISelector`1 targetConstraintsCert, ISelector`1 targetConstraintsAttrCert)
   at MimeKit.Cryptography.BouncyCastleSecureMimeContext.BuildCertificateChain(X509Certificate certificate)
   at MimeKit.Cryptography.TemporarySecureMimeContext.GetCmsSigner(MailboxAddress mailbox, DigestAlgorithm digestAlgo)
   at MimeKit.Cryptography.BouncyCastleSecureMimeContext.Sign(...)
   ...
```

- `CanSign` and `CanEncrypt` return `true`.
- Manually building the X509Chain works fine, indicating the certificates are valid and trusted.

`ApplicationPkcs7Mime.Sign(ctx, secureSigner, DigestAlgorithm.Sha256, bodyText);` same exception.

`ApplicationPkcs7Mime.Sign(ctx, new CmsSigner(senderCert) { DigestAlgorithm = DigestAlgorithm.Sha256 }, bodyText);` works.

title: InternetAddressList.TryParse unexpected behaviour
author: Nowak-Kacper
body:
**Describe the bug**
I have a unit test in which I discovered potential bug in InternetAddressList.TryParse(ParserOptions, String, InternetAddressList).
For input String:
`
@"
			<test@example.com>,
			test@example.com,
			""Test Example"" <test@example.com>,
			""Test Example (test@example.com)"" <test@example.com>,
			""Trims Whitespaces   "" <trims@whitespaces.com >,
			""Test Example""
			"
`
I'm getting 6 email addresses, but when I modify it to:

`
@"
			""Test Example"",
			<test@example.com>,
			test@example.com,
			""Test Example"" <test@example.com>,
			""Test Example (test@example.com)"" <test@example.com>,
			""Trims Whitespaces   "" <trims@whitespaces.com >
			"
`
I'm getting 5 email addresses. First(""Test Example"") and second(<test@example.com>) are merged into one.

**Platform:**
 - OS: Windows 10
 - .NET Runtime: CoreCLR
 - .NET Framework:  .NET 9.0
 - MimeKit Version: 4.7.1

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. run InternetAddressList.TryParse(ParserOptions, String, InternetAddressList) with strings that I pasted above.
2. compare results from both strings

**Expected behavior**
I expect to get same result which is different only in order.

**Code Snippets**

`ParserOptions options = ParserOptions.Default.Clone();
		options.AddressParserComplianceMode = RfcComplianceMode.Looser;
		options.ParameterComplianceMode = RfcComplianceMode.Looser;
		options.Rfc2047ComplianceMode = RfcComplianceMode.Looser;
		return options;`

title: National character sets not decoded properly
author: klasyc
body:
**Describe the bug**
I am using MailKit to download messages from IMAP server. Until the messages are encoded in UTF-8, everything works fine. But when there is a message encoded with single-byte encoding, such as ISO-8859-2, both subject and message body display the national characters wrongly.

**Platform (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Windows 10
 - .NET Runtime: CoreCLR
 - .NET Framework: .NET 9
 - MimeKit Version: 4.11

**To Reproduce**
Use the code snippet below + attached example of email message. The attached file is a sample message created with Outlook 2019, which by default prefers national encodings to UTF-8. It displays properly in Thunderbird and Roundcube, but it's broken when loaded with MailKit.

**Expected behavior**
As soon as the message has correct headers, MailKit should recognize encoding and return properly decoded strings.

**Code Snippets**
```csharp
using var fileStream = File.OpenRead("mail.txt");
var msg = MimeMessage.Load(fileStream);
Console.WriteLine("Subject: " + msg.Subject);
Console.WriteLine("Html: " + msg.HtmlBody);
```

[mail.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20724721/mail.txt)

title: Attachments with UTF-8 characters in name are not displayed properly when FormatOption.International = true
author: TheoLesieux
body:
When sending a mail with attachment names having special characters (here **Devólución** and **Devolución**), I noticed that they are not displayed properly 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/537a368d-a45c-41dc-b78c-a28441df7a81)

After some research I found that this issue came after the version 4.7 where I think it is related to this commit https://github.com/jstedfast/MimeKit/commit/5bdb9f0c58044a4318ed79d907276ac875267507#diff-6a354989551d7087a1ef00719626fb9b6317e1877da620572c8cb45b37917e6a where UTF8 encryption is not enforced anymore. 

In the eml extracted before the send async it is noted as ISO 8859-1 and the eml contains the things correctly encoded. As it should be with F3 (the first  "ó") **name*=iso-8859-1''Dev%F3luci%F3n.pdf**
After the send in the mailbox the eml now encodes as UTF8 C3 B3 but with the encoding still stated as ISO 8859-1 leading to the wrong output **name="=?iso-8859-1?Q?Devoluci=C3=B3n.pdf?="**

This happens only for FormatOption.International = true

Do you know how I could resolve this issue with the existing features of MimeKit, I couldn't find something to fix it

title: Strong-named assembly
author: danielklecha
body:
Hi!

My project is using strong-named assemblies.
Could you modify the deployment to generate two versions of the package?
For example:
- MimeKitLite and MimeKitLite.StrongName
- MimeKit and MimeKit.StrongName
Do you have plans to do that?

If you need to generate an SNK file, you can follow these steps:
1. Open the Visual Studio Developer Command Prompt
2. Run the following command: `sn -k MimeKit.snk`
3. Store the generated file in a safe place.
4. If you need to use it with GitHub Actions as a secret, you can generate a base64 version with the following PowerShell command: `[Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('MimeKit.snk'))`

I use GitHub actions for my library, and steps similar to those below should work for your library.

```yaml
    - name: Create Strong Name Assembly Key File for MimeKit
      run: |
        echo "${{ secrets.STRONG_NAME_KEY_MIMEKIT }}" | base64 --decode > MimeKit.snk

    - name: Create Strong Name Assembly Key File for MimeKitLite
      run: |
        echo "${{ secrets.STRONG_NAME_KEY_MIMEKITLITE }}" | base64 --decode > MimeKitLite.snk

    - name: Build MimeKit (Strong-Named)
      run: dotnet build MimeKit/MimeKit.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore /p:SignAssembly=true /p:AssemblyOriginatorKeyFile=$PWD/MimeKit.snk

    - name: Pack MimeKit (Strong-Named)
      run: dotnet pack MimeKit/MimeKit.csproj --configuration Release --no-build /p:PackageId=MimeKit.StrongName

    - name: Build MimeKitLite (Strong-Named)
      run: dotnet build MimeKit/MimeKitLite.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore /p:SignAssembly=true /p:AssemblyOriginatorKeyFile=$PWD/MimeKitLite.snk

    - name: Pack MimeKitLite (Strong-Named)
      run: dotnet pack MimeKit/MimeKitLite.csproj --configuration Release --no-build /p:PackageId=MimeKitLite.StrongName

    - name: Publish to NuGet
      run: dotnet nuget push **/*.nupkg --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json --api-key ${{ secrets.NUGETAPIKEY }}
```

My configuration of the build action is here:
https://github.com/danielklecha/SharpIppNext/blob/master/.github/workflows/create-release.yml

Best regards

title: TemporarySecureMimeContext requires "keyEncipherment" key usage, even though this is not required by the spec
author: derekchoate
body:
Initial Problem
---

I purchased a SMIME certificate from Sectigo to test S/MIME email sending in an Azure Function, however, it threw a "CertificateNotFoundException" exception.

After some investigation, I discovered that the code required the "KeyEncipherment" key usage to be set, but my certificate didn't have this.

https://github.com/jstedfast/MimeKit/blame/917533650212bd5df7b3f0c22f7de83e493e1d92/MimeKit/Cryptography/TemporarySecureMimeContext.cs#L274

```
274: if (keyUsage != 0 && (keyUsage & X509KeyUsageFlags.KeyEncipherment) == 0)
275:   continue;
```

I then reviewed the standards and couldn't find any requirement that this be set, and it appears that Outllok, Gmail, iOS Mail don't require this to be set, so I propose to remove the check.

Standards
---

https://cabforum.org/working-groups/smime/requirements/#7123-subscriber-certificates

https://support.google.com/a/answer/7300887?hl=en#zippy=%2Cend-entity-certificate

Code Example
---

```
private static MimeMessage EncryptMessage(MimeMessage message, string certBase64)
{

    byte[] certBytes = Convert.FromBase64String(certBase64);
    var cert = X509CertificateLoader.LoadCertificate(certBytes) as System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate2;

    var bouncyCert = Org.BouncyCastle.Security.DotNetUtilities.FromX509Certificate(cert);

    using (var ctx = new TemporarySecureMimeContext())
    {
        ctx.Import(bouncyCert);
        message.Body = ApplicationPkcs7Mime.Encrypt(ctx, message.To.Mailboxes, message.Body);
    }

    return message;
}
```

Certificate Info
---
**Public Key Info**
> Algorithm=Elliptic Curve Public Key ( 1.2.840.10045.2.1 )
> Parameters=Elliptic Curve secp256r1 ( 1.2.840.10045.3.1.7 )
> Public Key=65 bytes: 04 94 90 7F F4 CB B4 A1 ...
> Key Size=256 bits
> Key Usage=Encrypt, Verify, Wrap, Derive

**Extension=Key Usage ( 2.5.29.15 )**
> Critical=YES
> Usage=Digital Signature, Key Agreement

**Extension=Extended Key Usage ( 2.5.29.37 )**
> Critical=NO
> Purpose #1=Email Protection ( 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.4 )
> Purpose #2=Client Authentication ( 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2 )

title: ByteArrayBuilder is not disposed in some places
author: daniel-lerch
body: `ByteArrayBuilder` is not disposed in `MimeAnonymizer.cs:449` and `MimeUtils.cs:575`. The internal buffer will therefore not be returned to the buffer pool in these cases. I believe these two cases are bugs.
